memberdef.argsstring: (DispatchingRule rule, string key, string fullName)[] RuleMap
memberdef.name: readonly

briefdescription.para: Mapping from DispatchingRule enum values to the short keys and full names used by the Python reference generator.

detaileddescription.para:
Each entry is a (rule, key, fullName) tuple where:
rule is the DispatchingRule enum value used to configure DESSimulator.
key is the abbreviated column identifier written to csharp_makespans.csv (e.g. "SPT"), matching the Python script's short-form keys.
fullName is the snake_case name written to the rule_full CSV column (e.g. "shortest_processing_time"), matching the Python script's full-form keys.

simplesect.para: Only rules present in this map are simulated and exported by RunAndExport.

briefdescription.para: Simulates all Taillard instances in a directory under every mapped rule and writes the results to CSV and JSON export files.

detaileddescription.para:
Processes files in the following sequence:
Discovers all *.json files in instanceDir, sorted alphabetically.
For each file, deserializes a TaillardInstance via Newtonsoft.Json. Files that fail to parse or produce null/incomplete data are skipped with a console warning; processing continues with the next file.
For each instance, iterates every entry in RuleMap, running a fresh DESSimulator per rule. Computes the optimality gap if TaillardMetadata.optimum is non-zero; otherwise leaves the gap column empty.
Appends one CSV row per instance/rule combination to an in-memory list.
Builds a per-instance/rule operation schedule list, sorted by start time, then machine ID, then job ID, and stores it under the key "{name}_{key}" in the schedules dictionary.
Writes csharp_makespans.csv to outputDir containing all CSV rows.
Writes csharp_schedules.json to outputDir containing all schedules, serialized with indented formatting for human readability.

simplesect.title: CSV columns
simplesect.para: instance, rule, rule_full, makespan, optimum, gap_pct, num_jobs, num_machines

simplesect.title: Schedule JSON structure
simplesect.para: A top-level object keyed by "{instanceName}_{ruleKey}". Each value is an array of operation objects with fields: job, op_index, machine, start, end, duration.

parameterdescription.para: Filesystem path to the directory containing Taillard .json files. Must be a real disk path — not a Unity Resources/ virtual path.
Filesystem path to the directory where output files will be written. Must exist before this method is called; CrossValidator.Start creates it if needed.

briefdescription.para: Exports makespan results and full operation schedules in the same format as the Python reference generator, enabling direct diff against job_shop_lib output.

detaileddescription.para:
Produces two output files whose column and key names are kept deliberately in sync with the Python reference scripts:
csharp_makespans.csv — one row per instance/rule combination, matching the column layout of reference_makespans.csv.
csharp_schedules.json — one entry per instance/rule combination containing the full sorted operation schedule, matching the structure of reference_schedules.json.
Usage as a standalone console app: ValidationExporter.RunAndExport("path/to/instances","path/to/output");

simplesect.para: Only the five rules present in RuleMap are exported. This subset matches the rules implemented in the Python reference generator; additional DispatchingRule values are intentionally excluded to keep the comparison columns aligned.
